title: Harden quick_query edge-case behavior
description: Why: runtime audits found concrete failure-mode and behavior-documentation gaps in quick_query(). What: fix deterministic failure handling and document/test surprising result shapes with focused follow-up work.

title: Quiet local tooling on PHP 8.4
description: Why: current dev quality commands pass on PHP 8.4 but emit vendor deprecation noise. What: upgrade or replace the affected tools in small, verifiable work items so local checks are trustworthy and quiet.

title: Handle PDO::prepare returning false in quick_query
description:
## Summary
`TypeDb\quick_query()` assumes `PDO::prepare()` always returns a statement. In `PDO::ERRMODE_SILENT`, invalid SQL causes `prepare()` to return `false`, and the library then crashes with `Error: Call to a member function execute() on false` instead of surfacing a useful database failure.

## Evidence
- Runtime path: `src/helpers.php:72-76`
- Reproduction command:
  `php -r 'require "src/SqlValue/SqlValue.php"; require "src/SqlValue/SqlString.php"; require "src/SqlValue/SqlInteger.php"; require "src/SqlValue/SqlFloat.php"; require "src/SqlValue/SqlNull.php"; require "src/Connection.php"; require "src/helpers.php"; $pdo=new PDO("sqlite::memory:"); $pdo->setAttribute(PDO::ATTR_ERRMODE, PDO::ERRMODE_SILENT); $conn=new TypeDb\\Connection($pdo); TypeDb\\quick_query($conn, "select * from", []);'`
- Observed result: `Error: Call to a member function execute() on false`

## Why It Matters
Consumers can configure PDO error mode, and the library claims to work with arbitrary PDO connections. Silent mode currently turns a SQL error into an internal type error-like crash path.

## Proposed Work
Detect `prepare()` failure and throw a deterministic exception that includes PDO error context, or otherwise document and enforce the required PDO error mode explicitly.
acceptance_criteria:
- `quick_query()` no longer calls `execute()` on `false`
- A regression test reproduces the `PDO::ERRMODE_SILENT` prepare failure path without mocks
- Failure surfaced to callers is deterministic and references the underlying SQL/PDO error context
notes:
Adjusted the regression test to avoid over-specifying SQLite's exact driver message while preserving the acceptance criteria. The test still uses a real sqlite::memory: PDO connection in ERRMODE_SILENT and invalid SQL, but now asserts the stable invariants of the RuntimeException message: it starts with 'Failed to prepare query [', includes bracketed PDO error context, contains a separator for the driver message, and ends with the original SQL text ('. SQL: select * from'). This keeps the failure deterministic and context-rich without depending on a specific SQLite/PDO wording across environments. Re-verified with vendor/bin/phpunit tests/QuickQueryTest.php, vendor/bin/phpunit tests, and a manual php -r invocation through quick_query(); all passed/behaved as expected aside from existing dependency deprecation warnings.
Merged reviewed worktree changes into the main workspace. Verified with composer install, ./vendor/bin/phpunit ./tests --testdox (clean under PHPUnit 9.6.34), ./vendor/bin/phpstan analyse, and a manual sqlite::memory: smoke test that now throws a deterministic RuntimeException with PDO error context when prepare() returns false in ERRMODE_SILENT.
close_reason: Implemented, reviewed, and verified in the main workspace.

title: Upgrade PHPStan for PHP 8.4 compatibility
description: Why: ./vendor/bin/phpstan analyse passes under PHP 8.4 but emits deprecation warnings from PHPStan 1.8.5 and dependencies bundled inside phpstan.phar, including nette/di, react/*, and symfony/string. What: upgrade PHPStan to a PHP 8.4-compatible release and verify analysis remains clean.
acceptance_criteria: Static analysis still passes at the current configured level after the upgrade.

title: Upgrade PHPUnit toolchain for PHP 8.4 compatibility
description: Why: Running the test suite under PHP 8.4 succeeds but emits deprecation warnings from vendor code in phpunit/phpunit 9.5.24 and sebastian/cli-parser 1.0.1. What: upgrade the PHPUnit stack to versions that are explicitly compatible with PHP 8.4, or otherwise pin an invocation strategy that removes PHP 8.4 deprecation noise without hiding real test failures.
acceptance_criteria: The chosen version constraints are recorded in composer.json/composer.lock and still run the existing test suite successfully.
notes:
Final minimization pass:
- Removed the global Composer 'config.platform.php = 8.1.0' override from composer.json because it broadened resolution policy repository-wide beyond the scope of this PHPUnit task.
- Kept the focused manifest constraints only: phpunit/phpunit '^9.6' plus explicit doctrine/instantiator '^1.5' in require-dev. The explicit instantiator constraint is the minimal targeted guard needed because PHPUnit 9.6 allows '^1.5 || ^2', and on a PHP 8.4 machine Composer otherwise prefers doctrine/instantiator 2.1.0, which requires PHP ^8.4 and breaks installability for contributors on PHP 8.1.
- Re-resolved after removing config.platform: 'composer update --with-all-dependencies phpunit/phpunit doctrine/instantiator' made no further dependency changes; 'composer update --lock' refreshed lock metadata.
- Verification: 'composer validate --no-check-publish' passes; './vendor/bin/phpunit ./tests --testdox' is still clean on PHP 8.4.1 with OK (64 tests, 64 assertions) and no vendor deprecation noise.
- Compatibility spot-check of the locked PHPUnit stack without global platform override: doctrine/instantiator 1.5.0 requires '^7.1 || ^8.0'; phpunit/phpunit 9.6.34 requires 'php >=7.3'; phpunit/php-code-coverage 9.2.32 requires 'php >=7.3'; sebastian/cli-parser 1.0.2 requires 'php >=7.3'; sebastian/exporter 4.0.8 requires 'php >=7.3'. No inspected PHPUnit-stack package in the resolved set requires PHP > 8.1.
- Worktree remains uncommitted for manager review.
Merged reviewed worktree changes into the main workspace. Verified with composer install, ./vendor/bin/phpunit ./tests --testdox, and confirmed the PHPUnit stack is now quiet on PHP 8.4 while keeping doctrine/instantiator locked to a PHP 8.1-compatible release.
close_reason: Implemented, reviewed, and verified in the main workspace.

title: Audit PHP 8.4 toolchain compatibility
description: Why: local quality commands succeed but emit deprecation noise under PHP 8.4. What: determine whether the warnings come from repository code, pinned tooling, or invocation choices, then propose focused remediation work.
acceptance_criteria: - identifies the source of the deprecation warnings\n- distinguishes repository issues from third-party tooling limits\n- proposes focused beads with practical acceptance criteria if remediation is warranted
notes:
Evidence gathered under PHP 8.4.1 after composer install:
- ./vendor/bin/phpunit ./tests --testdox passes (64 tests) but emits PHP 8.4 deprecations from vendor/sebastian/cli-parser 1.0.1 and vendor/phpunit/phpunit 9.5.24, plus related PHPUnit vendor packages (sebastian/exporter, ErrorHandler using E_STRICT).
- ./vendor/bin/phpstan analyse passes with no repo findings but emits PHP 8.4 deprecations from phpstan/phpstan 1.8.5 phar dependencies, notably nette/di DependencyChecker (ReflectionMethod::__construct with 1 arg), react/*, and symfony/string.
- ./vendor/bin/phpa ./src completes but emits PHP 8.4 deprecations from league/climate 3.8.2 and nikic/php-parser 4.15.1 used by rskuipers/php-assumptions 0.8.1.
- Direct repository smoke tests via php -d error_reporting=E_ALL -d display_errors=1 -r ... showed TypeDb\Connection, helper functions, and a create/insert/select flow work without deprecation output. This indicates the current noise is tooling/vendor compatibility, not application/library runtime code.
Created follow-up beads: type-db-kve (PHPUnit stack), type-db-crd (PHPStan), type-db-8zy (php-assumptions strategy).
Note: bd dolt pull failed in this isolated worktree with 'fetch from origin/main: Error 1105: no remote', so bead sync appears unavailable here.
close_reason: Audit completed and follow-up beads have been created and triaged.

title: Audit runtime behavior and test gaps
description: Why: the library is small enough for a targeted behavior audit, and we need concrete, delegable follow-up work. What: inspect the runtime API and tests for bugs, edge cases, or missing coverage, then record evidence and propose focused beads if needed.
acceptance_criteria: - inspects src/ and tests/ together\n- records concrete findings with file references or execution evidence\n- files follow-up beads only when they are specific and non-duplicate
notes: Final audit summary: reviewed src/Connection.php, src/helpers.php, src/SqlValue/*, tests/SqlValueTest.php, and tests/QuickQueryTest.php. Manually exercised quick_query()/to_sql()/from_sql() against sqlite::memory: using PHP CLI because vendor/ and phpunit are absent in this worktree and `composer test` is not defined. Confirmed normal round-trips for strings, ints, floats, and nulls. Findings: (1) concrete bug: in ERRMODE_SILENT, quick_query() crashes on `execute()` after prepare() returns false for invalid SQL (see follow-up bead type-db-1qj); (2) coverage gap: no tests for failing queries or the current unaliased scalar select shape `[[1 => SqlInteger(1)]]` from `select 1` (see follow-up bead type-db-ast). No repository files changed in this audit.
close_reason: Audit completed and follow-up beads have been created and triaged.

title: Audit the library for concrete delegable beads
description: Why: there is no existing queue of ready work. What: inspect the code, tests, docs, and execution workflow to identify small, verifiable tasks or bugs suitable for isolated worker agents.
acceptance_criteria: - identifies focused beads with clear acceptance criteria\n- avoids duplicate or overly generic work items\n- records evidence or code references for each proposed item
notes: Audit complete. Worker spikes type-db-n4u.3 and type-db-n4u.4 produced focused follow-up beads: runtime epic type-db-n4u.5 with type-db-1qj and type-db-ast, plus tooling epic type-db-n4u.6 with type-db-kve, type-db-crd, and type-db-8zy. Manual SQLite execution confirmed the library works on happy paths, while audits isolated a silent-mode prepare bug and PHP 8.4 vendor-tooling deprecation noise.
close_reason: Audit completed and follow-up beads have been created and triaged.

title: Coordinate agent worktrees and delegated triage
description: Why: establish a safe parallel-agent workflow for this repository. What: set up worktree prerequisites, audit the current library for focused work items, delegate ready beads to isolated agents, and verify whether the resulting changes actually work.

title: Create and ignore .worktrees for isolated agent work
description: Why: worker agents must use isolated worktrees managed through bd worktree commands. What: add .worktrees to .gitignore, create the directory, and verify the repository is ready for isolated branches.
acceptance_criteria: - .worktrees exists at the repo root\n- .gitignore ignores .worktrees\n- notes explain how worktrees should be named and created
notes:
Verified .worktrees exists at repo root and is ignored via .gitignore. Worktree names should cite bead IDs, and worker branches will be created with bd worktree create .worktrees/<name> --branch <branch-name>.
Main workspace now has .worktrees/ ignored and available for isolated worker branches. Worker sessions for type-db-1qj and type-db-kve used bd worktree create successfully.
close_reason: Implemented, reviewed, and verified in the main workspace.

title: Document and test quick_query row shape for unaliased selects
description: Why: quick_query("select 1") currently returns an array row keyed by 1, which is surprising and undocumented. What: add a non-mock integration test for this runtime shape and make the expected behavior explicit in tests or README so maintainers know whether it is intentional.
acceptance_criteria:
- an integration test covers the current row shape for select 1 using a real SQLite PDO connection
- the expected behavior is documented in test names or README
- the task does not duplicate prepare-failure handling covered by type-db-1qj
notes: Found during runtime/test audit in type-db-n4u.3. QuickQueryTest currently covers aliased selects (`select 1 as id`, `select 1 as `0``) and success writes/nulls, but not invalid SQL or an unaliased scalar select (`select 1`), which currently returns row key `1` at runtime.

title: Assess php-assumptions support on PHP 8.4
description: Why: ./vendor/bin/phpa ./src completes under PHP 8.4 but emits deprecation warnings from rskuipers/php-assumptions 0.8.1 and its dependencies league/climate 3.8.2 and nikic/php-parser 4.15.1. What: determine whether a dependency upgrade path exists for quiet PHP 8.4 execution, and if not, decide whether to replace or retire this tool from local quality checks.
acceptance_criteria: If the tool is kept, running ./vendor/bin/phpa ./src under PHP 8.4 no longer emits vendor deprecation warnings.

value: type-db quality gates: composer install --no-interaction, ./vendor/bin/phpunit ./tests --testdox, ./vendor/bin/phpstan analyse, ./vendor/bin/phpa ./src, plus manual sqlite::memory: smoke tests for runtime behavior.

type-db workflow: use bd for task tracking, create worker worktrees under .worktrees via 'bd worktree create .worktrees/<name> --branch <branch>', and remove them with 'bd worktree remove .worktrees/<name>'.
